column1: # Tests how for interacts with all other language features.
# Stack results only (no output capture). ERROR:substring for expected errors.
# for with integer count: body results accumulate
# Empty ranges (start >= end with positive step)
# Empty ranges (start <= end with negative step)
# === For + boolean logic (using parens for subexpressions) ===
# Two-arg if: condition true only sometimes
# === For + def (define before loop, use inside body) ===
# def inside loop body, use it in same body
for 3 [i var [[x] x mul x add 1]]
# Inner loop runs completely for each outer iteration
# Nested with arithmetic in inner body
# Break with body producing no results before break
# Continue on all iterations: no results
# Forward-first: stack fully satisfies add [int,int] before forward collection of 10
# === For result used in further computation ===
# Results from for feed into subsequent words
# Iterator does not leak after loop
# def inside loop body persists (runs on main engine stack)
# === For with body that uses both prefix and forward ===
